old_claim.note: Printing Press provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No printing dependency remains because soldiers can learn and execute coordinated volley drill through command and practice without printed manuals.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening description explains group volley use and matchlock operation without making print production part of the firing method.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Musketry volley effectiveness derives from organized groups firing together with gunpowder weapons.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A Gutenberg Bible proves movable-type printing existed but supplies no causal evidence for battlefield rank drill.

ontology_before: Musket formations depended directly on printing through an unrelated Gutenberg Bible source.
ontology_after: Musket volley formations depend on organized infantry and gunpowder firearms; printed manuals are optional dissemination context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: musketry_formations no longer directly depends on printing_press.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a specific drill system whose first implementation demonstrably required printed manuals.
short_reason: Volley drill does not require print production.
